commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Even of late my people have risen up as an enemy: you pull off the robe with the garment from them that pass by trustingly, as men returning from war. You have often stripped people of their necessary garments; and have treated such as were innocently passing on the way, as if they were at war with you. (Challoner) He alludes to Israel attacking Juda without cause, and killing 120,000 at once, while they took 200,000 women and children (ver. ) captives, whom Oded indeed persuaded them to release, 2 Paralipomenon xxviii. Septuagint are very obscure in this chapter.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: You have taken away: You have even stripped people of their necessary garments: and have treated such as were innocently passing on the way, as if they were at war with you.
